metric_name: EAI priority ranking for Global 2000 CIOs
metric_value: EAI and e-Commerce are the two most important CIO initiatives in the Global 2000 for 2000
notes: Aberdeen's editorial assessment of CIO priority landscape

metric_value: e-Commerce is the greatest challenge currently facing traditional EAI suppliers
notes: Aberdeen identifies eBI-EAI convergence as defining market force

metric_value: Convergence between e-Business Integration (eBI) and traditional EAI is reshaping best practices for the Extended Enterprise
notes: Core thesis: EAI vendors must incorporate eBI capabilities to survive

metric_value: 50 EAI supplier profiles in Third Edition (up from 19 in prior edition)
notes: Rapid market expansion: supplier count grew 163% between report editions

metric_value: EAI suppliers must evolve strategies to incorporate e-Business capabilities
notes: Aberdeen predicts traditional EAI vendors will need eBI transformation to survive

metric_value: EAI market will consolidate as e-Business demands reshape supplier landscape
notes: Report includes section on partnering strategies and market consolidations

metric_value: Report reviews 1998 predictions for 1999 and how they played out
notes: Self-assessment section confirms Aberdeen's track record on EAI predictions

metric_value: webMethods acquired by Software AG for $546M (2007); TIBCO taken private by Vista Equity (2014); IBM MQ remained dominant
notes: Market consolidated as predicted; major independent EAI vendors acquired or taken private

metric_value: Cloud integration platforms (MuleSoft founded 2006; Boomi founded 2000; Dell Boomi acquired 2010) fulfilled eBI vision; MuleSoft acquired by Salesforce for $6.5B in 2018
notes: Aberdeen's eBI convergence thesis proved correct; cloud/API integration became the modern EAI paradigm

metric_value: Report priced at $2000 for 203-page market study
notes: Premium pricing reflects high enterprise value of EAI market intelligence in 2000
